chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 3: The Eight Principles in Depth
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the third chapter of the IVD book. Deep dive on each
# of the eight IVD principles: one-line rule, explanation, why essential,
# how they work together, and common violations.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_1_foundations.chapters[2]
# Source:    ivd_system_intent.yaml (8 principles)
# Version: 1.6
# Created: 2026-01-26
# Status: Complete
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Explain each of the eight IVD principles in depth: one-line rule first, then why it is essential, what breaks without it, and how the principles work together as a system. Reader can apply and avoid common violations."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Knows each principle as a one-line, memorable rule (writing_style.principles_as_rules)
    2. Understands why each principle is essential—what breaks when it is violated
    3. Sees how the eight principles reinforce each other (system view)
    4. Can recognize common violations and how to avoid them
    5. Is ready for Chapter 4 (Intent Artifacts: The Core Mechanism) and Part II (practice)
    
    This chapter delivers the deep dive on principles that Chapter 2 only summarized. It is the reference for "why IVD works" and "what not to do."
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can state each principle in one line (or close) from memory
    - Reader can explain what breaks when a principle is violated (at least 3 principles)
    - Reader understands Principle 6 (AI as Understanding Partner) as the key for the AI Agents era
    - Reader knows when to use Principle 8 (Inversion) and when to skip it
    - Chapter ends with clear bridge to Chapter 4 (Intent Artifacts: The Core Mechanism)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1 purpose: establish the paradigm)"
    - "ivd_system_intent.yaml (canonical 8 principles)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch3)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "3.1"
      title: "Principle 1: Intent is Primary"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Intent is the primary artifact. Code and documentation are derived.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Intent describes WHAT/WHY; code describes HOW; docs explain the code"
        - "Why essential: what breaks without it (e.g. code becomes source of truth, docs drift, AI has nothing to verify against)

=================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch3)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "3.1"
      title: "Principle 1: Intent is Primary"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Intent is the primary artifact. Code and documentation are derived.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Intent describes WHAT/WHY; code describes HOW; docs explain the code"
        - "Why essential: what breaks without it (e.g. code becomes source of truth, docs drift, AI has nothing to verify against)"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: intent survives implementation, intent is technology-agnostic, intent is version-controlled"
        - "Common violations: intent added after code, intent describes HOW, intent in separate docs repo"
    
    - id: "3.2"
      title: "Principle 2: Understanding Must Be Executable"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Prose can be wrong silently. Executable understanding fails loudly.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Every claim verifiable through tests; constraints link to test files; evidence reproducible"
        - "Why essential: prose cannot be verified by AI; executable constraints catch hallucinations"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: constraints are executable, evidence is reproducible, metrics are measurable"
        - "Common violations: constraint without test path, prose claims without verification"
    
    - id: "3.3"
      title: "Principle 3: Bidirectional Synchronization"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Changes flow in any direction: Intent → Code → Docs → Intent, with verification.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Changes from intent, code, or docs; all trigger verification to prevent drift"
        - "Why essential: one-way flow (e.g. intent→code only) allows silent drift when code or docs change"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: intent changes propagate, code changes verify against intent, doc changes validate"
        - "Common violations: code change without running constraint tests, documentation contradicting intent"
    
    - id: "3.4"
      title: "Principle 4: Continuous Verification"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Verify alignment at every commit, every PR, every deploy.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Verification is not one-time; CI runs constraint tests; review cadence; drift detection"
        - "Why essential: one-time verification allows drift to compound; continuous catch catches misalignment early"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: automated verification (CI), manual review cadence, drift detection"
        - "Extension to evaluation: for systems that run repeatedly, continuous verification extends to continuous improvement — the experimental evaluation section (Ch6) formalizes evaluate → adjust → re-verify loops"
        - "Common violations: no automated constraint tests in CI, no review cadence, verification only at initial implementation"
    
    - id: "3.5"
      title: "Principle 5: Layered Understanding"
      required_content:
        - "One-line ru

drift to compound; continuous catch catches misalignment early"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: automated verification (CI), manual review cadence, drift detection"
        - "Extension to evaluation: for systems that run repeatedly, continuous verification extends to continuous improvement — the experimental evaluation section (Ch6) formalizes evaluate → adjust → re-verify loops"
        - "Common violations: no automated constraint tests in CI, no review cadence, verification only at initial implementation"
    
    - id: "3.5"
      title: "Principle 5: Layered Understanding"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Understanding has layers: Intent → Constraints → Rationale → Alternatives → Risks.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Each layer answers different questions; all layers verifiable, not just prose"
        - "Why essential: flat docs (only intent or only constraints) lose context; layers preserve why and what-if"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: intent layer, constraints layer, rationale, alternatives, risks (required fields)"
        - "Common violations: missing required layer, rationale without evidence, risk without monitoring strategy"
    
    - id: "3.6"
      title: "Principle 6: AI as Understanding Partner"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **AI writes the intent, implements against it, and verifies—not just syncs.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Key principle for AI Agents era; AI writes intent from human description, implements, verifies; catches own hallucinations"
        - "Why essential: without this, AI reads prose and guesses; with this, clarification at intent stage, turns drop, hallucinations caught at source"
        - "Workflow: You describe → AI writes intent → You review → AI implements → AI verifies"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: AI writes intent, AI implements against intent, AI verifies own work, linguistic mirroring as alignment signal"
        - "Teaching when user lacks knowledge: AI creates structured educational artifact explaining concepts, user confirms understanding, then proceeds (Step 0a, canonical)"
        - "Discovery when user can't describe: AI proposes goals/recipes/options, user picks, then intent flow (Step 0b, experimental)"
        - "Common violations: AI only implements (doesn't write intent), no verification step, unstructured prose (AI can't parse)"
    
    - id: "3.7"
      title: "Principle 7: Understanding Survives Implementation"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Implementation changes. Languages change. Intent persists.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Intent documents WHAT/WHY not HOW; technology-agnostic; survives rewrites and team changes"
        - "Why essential: intent coupled to implementation is lost when tech changes; agnostic intent transfers"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: intent documents behavior not implementation, constraints technology-agnostic, rationale explains context"
        - "Example: the interface section (Ch4)—documenting tool surface in intent so it survives implementation rewrite (e.g. Python→TypeScript); same tools, same intent. The experimental roles section ext

entation changes. Languages change. Intent persists.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: Intent documents WHAT/WHY not HOW; technology-agnostic; survives rewrites and team changes"
        - "Why essential: intent coupled to implementation is lost when tech changes; agnostic intent transfers"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: intent documents behavior not implementation, constraints technology-agnostic, rationale explains context"
        - "Example: the interface section (Ch4)—documenting tool surface in intent so it survives implementation rewrite (e.g. Python→TypeScript); same tools, same intent. The experimental roles section extends this to behavioral modes—documenting how an agent behaves in different contexts, independent of implementation."
        - "Common violations: intent tightly coupled to library, constraint tests implementation detail not behavior"
    
    - id: "3.8"
      title: "Principle 8: Innovation through Inversion"
      required_content:
        - "One-line rule first: **Innovation comes from inverting dominant beliefs—state the default, invert it, evaluate, implement.**"
        - "1–2 paragraphs: 4-step method (State Default, Invert, Evaluate, Implement); inversion_opportunities in intent artifacts"
        - "When to use: designing new/major intent; problem has conventional approach; stakes matter (perf, scale, security)"
        - "When to skip: small fix, no clear default, obvious solution good enough (per ivd_system_intent when_to_apply)"
        - "Sub-principles or examples: state the default, invert, evaluate, implement; document in intent when chosen"
        - "Common violations: inversion documented but intent contradicts chosen approach"
    
    - id: "3.9"
      title: "The System View: How Principles Reinforce Each Other"
      required_content:
        - "Principles work as a system, not independent rules"
        - "How Principle 1 (Intent Primary) enables Principle 2 (Executable): intent is the contract tests verify"
        - "How Principle 6 (AI Partner) depends on 1–5: AI needs structured intent, constraints, layers, verification"
        - "How Principle 8 (Inversion) feeds into Rationale and Alternatives (Principle 5)"
        - "Summary: reader sees the principles as one coherent framework, not a checklist"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 4: 'Next we turn to the artifact itself—what an intent artifact contains and where it lives.'"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points repeat or list the 8 one-line rules (principles_as_rules: repeat in chapter summaries)"
      - "Each principle section (3.1–3.8) has one-line rule in bold first, then 1–2 paragraphs (constraint: principles_as_rules)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines across chapter (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 4 (Intent Artifacts)"
      - "Optional: Checklist (e.g. violations to avoid, principles at a glance) where it adds value (book_system_intent.chapter_takeaways: where applicable)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_sys

"
      - "Key Points repeat or list the 8 one-line rules (principles_as_rules: repeat in chapter summaries)"
      - "Each principle section (3.1–3.8) has one-line rule in bold first, then 1–2 paragraphs (constraint: principles_as_rules)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines across chapter (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 4 (Intent Artifacts)"
      - "Optional: Checklist (e.g. violations to avoid, principles at a glance) where it adds value (book_system_intent.chapter_takeaways: where applicable)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch3; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Each principle is a one-line rule + explanation; why each is essential (what breaks without it)"
  - "The eight principles work together as a system, not independent rules"
  - "Principle 6 (AI writes intent, implements, verifies) is the key for the AI Agents era"
  - "Principle 6 extensions: teaching before intent (canonical), discovery before intent (experimental), linguistic mirroring"
  - "Common violations and how to avoid them; Principle 8: when to use inversion, when to skip"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "Each principle stateable as short, memorable rule; 1–2 quotable lines in chapter"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.principles_as_rules, quotable_principles"
    validation: "Manual: each of 8 principles has one-line rule in bold; at least one line quotable on its own"
    test: "Each section 3.1–3.8 starts with a bold one-line rule; at least one standalone bold sentence works as a tweet/pull-quote"
  
  - name: "principles_as_rules"
    requirement: "One-line rule first, then 1–2 paragraphs explanation (pattern: Clean Code, Stepdown)"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.principles_as_rules"
    validation: "Sections 3.1–3.8: first sentence or bold line is the rule; explanation follows"
    test: "For each section 3.1–3.8: the first non-heading line is a bold rule statement; followed by 1–2 explanation paragraphs"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_1"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    validation: "Manual: voice and tone match"
    test: "chapter.md uses second person ('you', 'your') as primary address; prose explains before examples illustrate"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow boo

the rule; explanation follows"
    test: "For each section 3.1–3.8: the first non-heading line is a bold rule statement; followed by 1–2 explanation paragraphs"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_1"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    validation: "Manual: voice and tone match"
    test: "chapter.md uses second person ('you', 'your') as primary address; prose explains before examples illustrate"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms and one-line rules, italic for emphasis, code for technical terms, paragraph structure (3–6 typical; >10 MUST be broken with subheadings)."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; italic for emphasis; code font for technical identifiers; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"
  
  - name: "canonical_principles"
    requirement: "Principle statements and when_to_use/skip (P8) must align with ivd_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "ivd_system_intent.yaml principles 1–8"
    validation: "One-line rules and when_to_apply for P8 match ivd_system_intent; no invented principles"
    test: "Each one-line rule in 3.1–3.8 matches the corresponding principle statement in ivd_system_intent.yaml; P8 when_to_apply matches"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml (canon consistency)"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch3 aligns with ivd_system_intent.yaml; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 3 is the deep dive on the eight principles; Ch2 gave overview only. Principles as one-line rules first, then explanation (principles_as_rules)."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part I purpose: establish the paradigm; Ch1 problem, Ch2 paradigm, Ch3 principles in depth, Ch4 artifacts.
    - Eight principles per ivd_system_intent.yaml (canon); book_system_intent aligned to 8 everywhere (fixed in v1.5).
    - Pattern from bestsellers (Clean Code, Pragmatic Programmer): principles as short rules, repeatable, memorable.
    - Principle 6 (AI as Understanding Partner) gets full weight—it is the AI Agents era differentiator.
    - Principle 8 (Inversion): include when to use and when to skip so readers don't over-apply.
    - Section 3.9 (System View) prevents "checklist" reading; principles reinforce each other.
    - Bridge to Ch4: reader now knows why; Ch4 is how (artifact structure, levels, first intent).
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1, Ch3 key_points, placeholder); ivd_system_intent

verywhere (fixed in v1.5).
    - Pattern from bestsellers (Clean Code, Pragmatic Programmer): principles as short rules, repeatable, memorable.
    - Principle 6 (AI as Understanding Partner) gets full weight—it is the AI Agents era differentiator.
    - Principle 8 (Inversion): include when to use and when to skip so readers don't over-apply.
    - Section 3.9 (System View) prevents "checklist" reading; principles reinforce each other.
    - Bridge to Ch4: reader now knows why; Ch4 is how (artifact structure, levels, first intent).
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1, Ch3 key_points, placeholder); ivd_system_intent.yaml (8 principles); research/best_selling_programming_books.md (principles_as_rules)"
  date: "2026-01-26"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Fewer principles (e.g., 3–5 core principles)"
    rejected_because: "All eight are load-bearing. Removing any weakens the system: without P3 (Bidirectional Sync), intent drifts from code. Without P8 (Inversion), IVD is verification-only, not innovation. The system view (3.9) shows why each matters."
    when_it_works: "Quick reference cards or executive summaries where brevity outweighs depth."

  - name: "Merge Ch3 principles into Ch2 paradigm overview"
    rejected_because: "Ch2 overview at ≤2 sentences per principle is already at capacity. Deep dive needs dedicated space: what breaks, common violations, sub-principles. Merging would bloat Ch2 past 40 pages."
    when_it_works: "If the book had only 5 principles or the reader already knew the paradigm."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Chapter becomes a dry reference list rather than a compelling narrative"
    action: "Each principle section: rule first, then concrete 'what breaks' scenario; 3.9 (System View) ties them into a story, not a checklist"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'could you feel why each principle matters?'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Principle statements drift from ivd_system_intent.yaml canon"
    action: "One-line rules must match ivd_system_intent.yaml statements exactly; constraint canonical_principles enforces this"
    monitor: "Cross-check during step_5"
    severity: "high"
  
  - condition: "Linguistic mirroring insufficiently covered in section 3.6"
    action: "Manuscript must include at least one concrete example of linguistic mirroring (AI echoes terms = alignment; AI substitutes = risk)"
    monitor: "Cross-check: grep for 'linguistic mirroring' or 'echoes' in chapter.md section 3.6"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-03-eight-principles/chapter_03_eight_principles_intent.yaml"

"Linguistic mirroring insufficiently covered in section 3.6"
    action: "Manuscript must include at least one concrete example of linguistic mirroring (AI echoes terms = alignment; AI substitutes = risk)"
    monitor: "Cross-check: grep for 'linguistic mirroring' or 'echoes' in chapter.md section 3.6"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-03-eight-principles/chapter_03_eight_principles_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-03-eight-principles/chapter.md"
  status: "complete"
  version: 2
  
  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_5_cross_check"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"
    notes: "Manuscript exists (172 lines, all 9 sections + Key Points). Content gap: section 3.6 missing explicit linguistic mirroring example. Cross-check pending."

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent and IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch3 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (3.1–3.9) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary; Key Points repeat or list the 8 one-line rules (principles_as_rules)"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, principles_as_rules, deliverables.manuscript.required_elements"
    - check: "Each section 3.1–3.8: one-line rule first (bold), then 1–2 paragraphs explanation"
      validates: "constraints.principles_as_rules"
    - check: "Principle statements align with ivd_system_intent.yaml; no contradictions (master_intent_alignment)"
      validates: "constraints.canonical_principles, constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    - check: "Principle 8 includes when to use and when to skip (from ivd_system_intent when_to_apply)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[3.8]"
    - check: "Section 3.9 explains how principles reinforce each other; bridge to Ch4"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[3.9]"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.5"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Section 3.4 (Principle 4): added evaluation forward reference — continuous improvement extends continuous verification. Updated manuscript with new paragraph in P4 section. Authorship forward reference was drafted for P6 but removed during audit — to

sections[3.8]"
    - check: "Section 3.9 explains how principles reinforce each other; bridge to Ch4"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[3.9]"
